info.name: Rogue
info.description: The Rogue additions to the Realms of Indoril game series.

description.p: Update your class to be compatible with the Realms of Indoril series of games. This will replace certain class features, add new ones, and augment existing ones.
Starting at 3rd level, once on each of your turns, when you deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature, you can reroll one of the Sneak Attack damage dice. You must use the new roll.
At 9th level, you can reroll up to two Sneak Attack damage dice instead.

sheet.p: You can reroll {{cunning precision:count}} of the Sneak Attack damage dice. You must use the new roll.

description.p: Starting at 3rd level, when a creature misses you a melee attack, you can move up to half your speed as a reaction. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks from the creature that missed you.

sheet.p: You can move up to half your speed as a reaction when a creature misses you with a melee attack. This does not provoke opportunity attacks from the creature that missed you.

description.p: Starting at 3rd level, you can take the Use an Object action as a bonus action.
If you gain this feature from the Thief subclass, you can instead use that bonus action to take the Use an Object action or make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check.

sheet.p: You can take the Use an Object action as a bonus action.

description.p: Beginning at 11th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

sheet.description: You can attack two times, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

description.p: Beginning at 13th level, your ability to punish openings becomes even more lethal. Once per round, when a creature you can see within your weapon's range is hit by an attack made by another creature, you can use your reaction to make one weapon attack against the target.

If the attack hits, you can apply your Sneak Attack to the attack, even if the normal Sneak Attack conditions are not met. You cannot deal Sneak Attack damage with this feature if you already dealt Sneak Attack damage on the same turn.

sheet.description: You may attack, and if it has not been used this turn, Sneak Attack, a creature that is hit within your range by another creature.

description.p: If you take a subclass that grants Spellcasting, the number of spells you may prepare increases by 2
